channel.title: Ninad Pathak
channel.description: Programmer-turned-writer helping DevTools and SaaS companies create technical content their engineering teams actually approve.

item.title: Speculative decoding explained: how LLMs generate tokens faster without changing the output
item.description: Speculative decoding cuts LLM latency by 2-3x without changing output quality. Here's how the draft-verify loop works, why acceptance rate is the only number that matters, and when it actually hurts.

item.title: Best LLMs for coding in 2026 (it's not what benchmarks say)
item.description: HumanEval scores are nearly meaningless in 2026. Here's how to pick the right coding LLM based on your actual task: agentic coding, autocomplete, or cost-sensitive production.

item.title: Agent harnesses: the infrastructure layer your LLM agent actually needs
item.description: Every production AI agent needs a harness. Here's what one contains, why frameworks often aren't enough, and how to build the layer that actually determines reliability.

item.title: The Model Context Protocol explained: why MCP matters architecturally
item.description: A practical explanation of the Model Context Protocol, why it matters architecturally, how FastAPI MCP fits in, and how to avoid framework lock-in in agent systems.

item.title: Time to first token: what TTFT measures and how to reduce it
item.description: TTFT is the latency metric that matters most for chat, copilots, and interactive AI apps. Here is what time to first token measures and how to reduce it without optimizing the wrong thing.

item.title: Prompt caching: what it is and when the math works in your favor
item.description: Prompt caching can cut latency and input cost, but only when your prompts share a stable prefix often enough to make cache reuse real. Here is how I think about it in production.

item.title: RAG vs fine-tuning: how to actually decide
item.description: RAG and fine-tuning solve different problems. Here is how I decide between them, when to combine them, and where teams waste time choosing the wrong one.
